channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI’s Studio Ghibli-style images renew the debate over AI and copyright
item.description: This week, the internet had a rare opportunity to transform its selfies and family photos into stunning Studio Ghibli “portraits.” What started as a lighthearted trend quickly took a darker turn.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk sells X to his own xAI for $33 billion in all-stock deal
item.description: Elon Musk has sold social media site X to his own xAI artificial intelligence company in a $33 billion all-stock deal, the billionaire announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why the ‘iPhone of smartwatches’ remains so elusive
item.description: Ever since it became obvious that the iPhone was one of the most transformative consumer products in history, a question has been floating out there: Would its impact ever be matched by a device in an even newer category? And if something pulled it off, what would it be? After a decade and a half—so
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: This may be the most private and secure cloud-based AI solution around
item.description: What if you could build a business-specific generative AI (genAI) solution that was hosted in the cloud? What if that cloud used the most energy-efficient systems in the world? And what if those services were also all running on the world’s most secure computing platform, the Mac? Wonder no more, b
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Politics of Dating: How Trump's Return Has Changed the Way Americans Connect
item.description: The return of Donald Trump to office has led to a surge in niche dating apps, with both left and right-wing platforms gaining popularity. But while the right has coalesced around its own dating enclave, the left remains fragmented and resistant to building its own space.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Signal chat discussion of 'clearly classified' information violated law, experts say
item.description: The inadvertent inclusion of a journalist on a Signal group chat discussing attack plans means officials likely violated the Espionage Act and public records laws while flouting guidance on how to discuss sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: With the rise of genAI, it’s time to follow Apple’s Security Recommendations
item.description: Apple’s Safari browser has a really useful password management feature, which is now also available as a standalone app called Passwords.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Schumer, Democrats call for full, unredacted transcript of Signal attack chat
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and his Democratic colleagues are calling for a full, unredacted transcript of the Trump administration’s Signal group chat. “Yesterday’s revelations were alarming, but they were not enough.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Arista COO launches NextHop AI for customized networking infrastructure
item.description: A new player in the networking industry is tackling the unique challenges of AI infrastructure at hyperscale.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How RamaLama helps make AI model testing safer
item.description: Consider this example: An amazing new software tool emerges suddenly, turning technology industry expectations on their heads by delivering unprecedented performance at a fraction of the existing cost.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Signalgate: There’s an IT lesson here
item.description: You know how IT admins are always warning employees about best practices for security? They’re always mandating which apps to use, which to avoid and which devices can safely connect to corporate networks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No application can eliminate human error: Signal’s head defends the app
item.description: When the editor-in-chief of The Atlantic, Jeff Goldberg, was accidentally added to a Signal conversation, things took a surprising turn.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state
item.description: GSA's acting Administrator Stephen Ehikian said that the agency is breaking down the data silos to allow systems to better communicate with one another. The post Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 5 big takeaways from Nvidia GTC
item.description: Now that the dust has settled from Nvidia’s GTC 2025, a few industry experts weighed in on some core big picture developments from the conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
